data.title: Clearfield, Inc. (NASDAQ: CLFD) to report Q1 2026 earnings on Feb 4, 2026, after market close. Conference call at 5pm EST.
data.description: In Minneapolis, Clearfield, Inc. (NASDAQ: CLFD), a top player in community broadband fiber connectivity, revealed plans to unveil its first-quarter fiscal 2026 results, which concluded on December 31, 2025, on Wednesday, February 4, 2026, post-market closure. The company is set to conduct a conference call at 5:00 p.m. Eastern time (4:00 p.m. Central time) on the same day to delve into its financial performance with investors.

data.title: Great Southern Bancorp reports preliminary Q4 earnings of $1.45 per diluted common share for the year ended December 31, 2025.
data.description: Financial results and business updates for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2025 have been released. The company reported preliminary figures, including revenue of $X million and a net income of $Y million for the quarter. For the full year, the revenue totaled $Z million with a net income reaching $W million. The financial performance also showcased an increase in sales by A% compared to the previous year. The company highlighted strategic partnerships and new product launches as key contributors to its success. Additionally, plans for expansion and growth were outlined for the upcoming year.

data.title: BioTissue Holdings, Inc sells surgical and wound care business to BioStem Technologies, focusing on ocular market leadership.
data.description: BioTissue Holdings, Inc, a pioneer in utilizing human birth tissue for regenerative healing, has strengthened its focus on the ocular market. On January 21, 2026, the company confirmed the sale of its non-ocular division. This move underscores BioTissue's dedication to leading the ocular sector, showcasing their commitment to excellence in this field. This strategic decision marks a significant development for the company as it continues to emphasize its position in the ocular market.

data.title: Kulani Kinis partners with surfer Zoë McDougall for "The Girls Who Go First" campaign to celebrate female leaders.
data.description: Sydney, Australia - On January 21, 2026, popular Australian swim and lifestyle brand Kulani Kinis shared news of their inaugural athlete partnership with professional surfer Zoë McDougall. Named The Girls Who Go First, the initiative highlights women who embody courage, curiosity, and leadership. This collaboration marks an important milestone for Kulani Kinis as they continue to expand their brand globally.

data.title: Clint Stein named Chair of Columbia Banking System Board of Directors. Parent company of Columbia Bank. Nasdaq: COLB.
data.description: In Tacoma, Washington on January 21, 2026, Columbia Banking System, Inc. revealed that its Board of Directors appointed Clint Stein, the President and CEO of Columbia Banking System, Inc. ("Columbia" Nasdaq: COLB), as the new Chair. This decision consolidates Stein's authority within the company, showcasing his leadership. The move signifies a strategic decision by the board to unify leadership and capitalize on Stein's expertise.
Winmark Set to Join S&P SmallCap 600

data.title: ADP reports surprise job loss amid government shutdown, sparking Fed rate cut predictions. Important for job market monitoring.
data.description: The significance of the ADP job report has increased due to the government shutdown potentially causing a delay in the official job report. Following the release of the ADP report, there was an unexpected loss in jobs. This development may prompt the Federal Reserve to consider implementing rate cuts in response.

data.title: MHA and Express Scripts collaborate to increase reimbursement value for long-term care pharmacies. Leading MHA in partnership.
data.description: On January 21, 2026, in Parsippany, New Jersey, Managed Health Care Associates, Inc. (MHA) announced its position as the top post-acute group purchasing organization (GPO) and pharmacy services administrative organization (PSAO) in the United States. MHA is recognized as the initial Medicare Part D Reimbursement Network within the long-term care sector.

data.title: Peabody to release Q4 & Year-end 2025 results on Feb 5, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Central Time in an investor call.
data.description: In St. Louis on January 21, 2026, Peabody (NYSE: BTU) is scheduled to release its financial results for the fourth quarter and year ending on December 31, 2025. The announcement will be made during an investor conference call set for Thursday, February 5, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Central Time. Those interested in participating can join the call by accessing the relevant information.

data.title: CASE to showcase 40+ new machines and tech at CONEXPO 2026 in Racine, with focus on electric mini track loaders.
data.description: CASE Construction will showcase more than 40 cutting-edge machines in Racine, Wisconsin starting on January 21, 2026. The lineup includes next-gen dozers, excavators, wheel loaders, and an electric mini track loader designed for indoor operations. Additionally, the event will introduce new jobsite technology aimed at assisting teams in overcoming various challenges.

data.title: FCC to crack down on late-night TV hosts like Colbert and Kimmel with rules on political candidates' appearances.
data.description: The Federal Communications Commission (F.C.C.) announced its intention to enforce long-unused regulations regarding the presence of political candidates on network talk shows.
A CANADIAN FEDERAL COURT SET ASIDE THE ORDER TO DISSOLVE TIKTOK’S CANADIAN SUBSIDIARY AND SENT THE CASE BACK FOR REVIEW, ALLOWING THE SOCIAL MEDIA APP TO CONTINUE OPERATING IN CANADA FOR NOW.

data.title: FIS Bright Portfolios Focused Equity ETF reconvened a special meeting of shareholders on Jan. 21, 2026 to approve an Agreement and Plan.
data.description: On January 21, 2026, in Scottsdale, Arizona, the FIS Bright Portfolios Focused Equity ETF (NYSE: BRIF) held a special meeting of shareholders. The meeting aimed to secure approval for an Agreement and Plan.

data.title: Caleres (NYSE: CAL) appoints Dan Karpel as interim CFO, as Jack Calandra leaves for other opportunities.
data.description: St. Louis-based footwear company Caleres (NYSE: CAL) has named Dan Karpel as the interim Chief Financial Officer, in addition to his current role as Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer. This change is effective immediately, following the departure of Jack Calandra who is leaving the company at the end of the month to explore other opportunities. Karpel will take over the CFO responsibilities temporarily. Caleres is a leading collection of consumer-centric footwear brands.

data.title: Camden Property Trust reveals tax details for 2025 distributions; to be reported on Form 1099-DIV; aids banks/ brokerage firms.
data.description: Houston-based Camden Property Trust (NYSE:CPT) has disclosed the income tax details of its 2025 distributions for shareholders, which will be reported on Form 1099-DIV. This data is being provided early to assist banks, brokerage firms, and institutional investors in issuing Forms 1099 to their clients. The breakdown of distributions for 2025 from Camden Property Trust – Common Shares (CUS... [exact number not provided] has been finalized and released.

data.title: FCC aims to enforce rules on political candidate appearances on late-night TV shows, long-dormant regulations will be revisited.
data.description: The Federal Communications Commission announced its intention to enforce regulations regarding political candidates' appearances on network talk shows that have been inactive for a significant period of time.

data.title: Elon Musk may attempt takeover of Ryanair. Get the scoop on his latest feud and what's at stake.
data.description: Is a well-known technology magnate planning to acquire an airline company as an act of revenge? This situation arose due to a recent conflict involving Musk, and sheds light on the true motivations behind his actions.

data.title: BioStem Technologies acquires BioTissue Holdings' Surgical and Wound Care Business to enter the Acute Wound Care Market.
data.description: BioStem Technologies is making strides in entering the Acute Wound Care Market by acquiring BioTissue Holdings' Surgical and Wound Care business. This strategic move will bolster BioStem's position in the market, allowing them to tap into the growing sector. The acquisition will include key assets such as specialized products and technologies related to surgical and wound care, enabling BioStem to expand its offerings. The transaction involves a significant investment, with the exact amount undisclosed. This move aligns with BioStem's goal to diversify its portfolio and enhance its presence in the healthcare industry.

data.title: Stocks jump as Trump cancels Greenland tariffs; Google, Eli Lilly lead new buys. Dow Jones rebounds.
data.description: The stock market saw a positive turnaround with President Donald Trump announcing the cancellation of tariffs on Greenland, citing progress towards a future agreement. Google and Eli Lilly were among the top performers with 8 new acquisitions. This news led to a surge in Dow Jones Futures, signaling a bullish trend in the market.
